preamble: The shift from the diphthong [au] to a nasalized vowel [õ] is a common historical development, involving smoothing of the diphthong and nasalization of the resulting vowel.

examples.note: Historical development of the Portuguese -ão ending, involving stages of nasalization and smoothing from Latin -anum/-onum/um sequences.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Languages: A Historical Introduction. Cambridge University Press.
